msDesc.desc: wall painting of martyrdom of St Edmund on eastern end of northern wall of nave
Tomb of Thomas Knolles
Not extant; formerly at entrance to great west door
Monumental brass for Robert Hylton, yeoman of guard to Henry VIII, 1523
Monumental brass inscription in 8 English black letter verses for John Pynnoke, first priest of the chantry founded by Sir John Norbury, 1521
Monumental brass inscription for Sir William Martyn, 1524
A chrysom brass inscribed with 2 English verses
Monumental brass effigy in civilian dress with foot inscription in three lines of English verse, not dated but c. 1505
Incised slab to William Farnham (d. 1548)
Alabaster slab for Ralph Wodford esq (d. 1498)
Not extant; monumental brass for priest in mass vestments with inscription in 4 English verses; c. 1430
1535; 1. Window glazed at expense of Robert Ferrour, mayor of Norwich in 1526 and 1536, and Robert Jannys, mayor of Norwich in 1517 and 1524

msIdentifier.repository: Formerly in townhall (now in Axbridge Museum)

msDesc.desc: Stained glass roundel with nimbed dove descending in a glory with a scroll; (early sixteenth century?)
Not extant; dedicatory inscription in east window of south (Lady) chapel; n.d. (probably fifteenth century)
Eastern window of south aisle over the portrait of a person praying
Carved inscription around wooden tomb of Sir John Savile, (d. 1505), and his two wives Alice (Vernon) (d. 1493) and Elizabeth (Paston), who was a second cousin of King Henry VII; 1529
Tomb of Alice Cresacre
Palimpsest monumental brass appropriated for Thomas Sparrow, d. 1513; effigy of man in civilian dress with 3-line Latin foot inscription; English couplet in prayer scroll
Monumental brass for Brian Rouclyff (d. 1494) & Joanne his wife
Monumental brass for Sir Richard Wryght (1515)
Probably a monumental brass for William Clarrell and wife Margaret; 1460
Monumental brass for Thomas Middleton of Kirkby Overblow, brother of Nicholas Midleton and wife Joan, (daughter of Sir William Plumpton)
Probably a monumental brass (1505)

msIdentifier.settlement: Godstow Oxfordshire (excavated at the Benedictine nunnery at Godstow, Oxfordshire, before 1868)

msDesc.desc: A broad hoop engraved on the outer side with flowers and leaves. Within this are lozenge-shaped panels, engraved with the Trinity, the Virgin and Child, and a bearded male saint
Black letter inscription on the inner surface. From the uncatalogued collection of Joan Evans
Carved memorial in church porch; late fifteenth century
Transcription in BL, MS Add. 36295, f.17r [MS owned by John Anstis, antiquarian (1669 –1744)]

Not extant; monumental brass for Sir Philip Tilney, husband of Isabel, daughter and coheiress of Sir Edmund Thorp of Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk. After her death he became a secular canon and was admitted to St Botolph's prebend in Lincoln Cathedral where he was buried in 1453
Small incised slab
Monumental brass for John Toddenham, c. 1450
Stained glass panel; provenance unknown; probably early sixteenth century
Stained glass panel; provenance unknown; probably early sixteenth century
Stained glass panel; provenance unknown; probably early sixteenth century
Stained glass panel; provenance unknown; probably early sixteenth century
On the third pier of the south arcade
Inscription in wood on the south side of the chancel
